title: Compensation and insurance payments
description: Check if you need to declare and pay tax on compensation and insurance payments, including settlements.
keywords: Compensation payments, insurance payments, personal injury compensation, compensation, settlement payments, redress scheme, conflicted remuneration

child_links.title: Personal injury compensation structured settlements
child_links.description: A structured settlement you receive as compensation for a personal injury is tax exempt if it meets certain conditions.

child_links.title: Compensation from financial institutions or under a scheme
child_links.description: When tax applies to a compensation payment from a financial institution or under the CSLR.

child_links.title: 2019 Robodebt class action settlement payments
child_links.description: A settlement payment you receive from the 2019 Robodebt class action is not taxable.

child_links.title: Redress scheme payments
child_links.description: Redress scheme payments made as compensation or damages for a wrong, injury or illness are non-assessable income.

child_links.title: Payments of conflicted remuneration
child_links.description: Payments of conflicted remuneration you receive may need to be included as income in your tax return.

child_links.title: Income protection insurance payments
child_links.description: Declare payments from an income protection, sickness or accident insurance policy in your tax return.
